TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_balaut, title = balaut, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/balaut} }

S.FORM: == balaut == Ťõô yadah ku balaut i laku nu niyam. siwkay nu balat balaut sa, nu Pangcah a kamu sa, u "takula". maazih tu i sawac i tukus i buyu. sademiad sa maazih, yadah i niyazu'. taneng yu mukan. asu' tu ku titi, mahiza tu tulakuk. u kabi' sa kapah tu ukak nu tademaw. anu mapetek ku ukak, mukan tuyniyan a kabi'. tinaku a kamu mibalaut śäďťĚíŤõô mibalaut kita i laku. mibalautay śäďťĚíŤõôÁöĄ kau kisu Taymu ku mibalautay i laku. pibalauti ŚéĽśäďťĚíŤõô pibalauti tu sakalabi nu mita! sibalaut śúČťĚíŤõô sibalaut ku lakulakuan nu pongkang. sibalautay śúČťĚíŤõôÁöĄ ni Taymu a pubu ku sibalautay. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika A frog is any member of a diverse and largely carnivorous group of short-bodied, tailless amphibians composing the orderAnura (Ancient Greek an-, without + oura, tail). The oldest fossil "proto-frog" appeared in the early Triassic of Madagascar, but molecular clock dating suggests their origins may extend further back to the Permian, 265 million years ago. Frogs are widely distributed, ranging from the tropics to subarctic regions, but the greatest concentration of species diversity is in tropical rainforests. There are approximately 4,800 recorded species, accounting for over 85% of extant amphibian species. They are also one of the five most diverse vertebrate orders.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺaut == * jadańß ku bar~…ĺaut i r~…ĺaku nu nijam. siwkaj nu bar~…ĺat bar~…ĺaut sa, nu paŇč ¶ańß a kamu sa, u "takur~…ĺa". maazińß tu i sawa ¶ i tukus i buju. sad…ômiad sa maazińß, jadańß i nijazu Ē~ °. tan…ôŇč ju mukan. asu Ē~ ° tu ku titi, mańßiza tu tur~…ĺakuk. u kabi Ē~ ° sa kapańß tu ukak nu tad…ômaw. anu map…ôt…ôk ku ukak, mukan tujnijan a kabi Ē~ °. tinaku a kamu mibar~…ĺaut *** mibar~…ĺaut kita i r~…ĺaku. mibar~…ĺautaj **** kau kisu tajmu ku mibar~…ĺautaj i r~…ĺaku. pibar~…ĺauti **** pibar~…ĺauti tu sakar~…ĺabi nu mita! sibar~…ĺaut *** sibar~…ĺaut ku r~…ĺakur~…ĺakuan nu poŇčkaŇč. sibar~…ĺautaj **** ni tajmu a pubu ku sibar~…ĺautaj. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika a frog is anj m…ômb…ôr of a di*…ôrs…ô and r~…ĺarg…ôr~…ĺj ¶arni*orous group of sńßort-bodi…ôd, tair~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôss ampńßibians ¶omposiŇč tńß…ô ord…ôranura (an ¶i…ônt gr…ô…ôk an-, witńßout + oura, tair~…ĺ). tńß…ô or~…ĺd…ôst fossir~…ĺ "proto-frog" app…ôar…ôd in tńß…ô …ôarr~…ĺj triassi ¶ of madagas ¶ar, but mor~…ĺ…ô ¶ur~…ĺar ¶r~…ĺo ¶k datiŇč sugg…ôsts tńß…ôir origins maj …ô*t…ônd furtńß…ôr ba ¶k to tńß…ô p…ôrmian, *** mir~…ĺr~…ĺion j…ôars ago. frogs ar…ô wid…ôr~…ĺj distribut…ôd, raŇčiŇč from tńß…ô tropi ¶s to subar ¶ti ¶ r…ôgions, but tńß…ô gr…ôat…ôst ¶on ¶…ôntration of sp…ô ¶i…ôs di*…ôrsitj is in tropi ¶ar~…ĺ rainfor…ôsts. tńß…ôr…ô ar…ô appro*imat…ôr~…ĺj *,*** r…ô ¶ord…ôd sp…ô ¶i…ôs, a ¶ ¶ountiŇč for o*…ôr **% of …ô*tant ampńßibian sp…ô ¶i…ôs. tńß…ô ar…ô ar~…ĺso on…ô of tńß…ô fi*…ô most di*…ôrs…ô *…ôrt…ôbrat…ô ord…ôrs.
S.FORM: == balaut == Ťõô yadah ku balaut i laku nu niyam. siwkay nu balat balaut sa, nu Pangcah a kamu sa, u "takula". maazih tu i sawac i tukus i buyu. sademiad sa maazih, yadah i niyazu'. taneng yu mukan. asu' tu ku titi, mahiza tu tulakuk. u kabi' sa kapah tu ukak nu tademaw. anu mapetek ku ukak, mukan tuyniyan a kabi'. tinaku a kamu mibalaut śäďťĚíŤõô mibalaut kita i laku. mibalautay śäďťĚíŤõôÁöĄ kau kisu Taymu ku mibalautay i laku. pibalauti ŚéĽśäďťĚíŤõô pibalauti tu sakalabi nu mita! sibalaut śúČťĚíŤõô sibalaut ku lakulakuan nu pongkang. sibalautay śúČťĚíŤõôÁöĄ ni Taymu a pubu ku sibalautay. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika A frog is any member of a diverse and largely carnivorous group of short-bodied, tailless amphibians composing the orderAnura (Ancient Greek an-, without + oura, tail). The oldest fossil "proto-frog" appeared in the early Triassic of Madagascar, but molecular clock dating suggests their origins may extend further back to the Permian, 265 million years ago. Frogs are widely distributed, ranging from the tropics to subarctic regions, but the greatest concentration of species diversity is in tropical rainforests. There are approximately 4,800 recorded species, accounting for over 85% of extant amphibian species. They are also one of the five most diverse vertebrate orders.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺaut == * jadańß ku bar~…ĺaut i r~…ĺaku nu nijam. siwkaj nu bar~…ĺat bar~…ĺaut sa, nu paŇč ¶ańß a kamu sa, u "takur~…ĺa". maazińß tu i sawa ¶ i tukus i buju. sad…ômiad sa maazińß, jadańß i nijazu Ē~ °. tan…ôŇč ju mukan. asu Ē~ ° tu ku titi, mańßiza tu tur~…ĺakuk. u kabi Ē~ ° sa kapańß tu ukak nu tad…ômaw. anu map…ôt…ôk ku ukak, mukan tujnijan a kabi Ē~ °. tinaku a kamu mibar~…ĺaut *** mibar~…ĺaut kita i r~…ĺaku. mibar~…ĺautaj **** kau kisu tajmu ku mibar~…ĺautaj i r~…ĺaku. pibar~…ĺauti **** pibar~…ĺauti tu sakar~…ĺabi nu mita! sibar~…ĺaut *** sibar~…ĺaut ku r~…ĺakur~…ĺakuan nu poŇčkaŇč. sibar~…ĺautaj **** ni tajmu a pubu ku sibar~…ĺautaj. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika a frog is anj m…ômb…ôr of a di*…ôrs…ô and r~…ĺarg…ôr~…ĺj ¶arni*orous group of sńßort-bodi…ôd, tair~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôss ampńßibians ¶omposiŇč tńß…ô ord…ôranura (an ¶i…ônt gr…ô…ôk an-, witńßout + oura, tair~…ĺ). tńß…ô or~…ĺd…ôst fossir~…ĺ "proto-frog" app…ôar…ôd in tńß…ô …ôarr~…ĺj triassi ¶ of madagas ¶ar, but mor~…ĺ…ô ¶ur~…ĺar ¶r~…ĺo ¶k datiŇč sugg…ôsts tńß…ôir origins maj …ô*t…ônd furtńß…ôr ba ¶k to tńß…ô p…ôrmian, *** mir~…ĺr~…ĺion j…ôars ago. frogs ar…ô wid…ôr~…ĺj distribut…ôd, raŇčiŇč from tńß…ô tropi ¶s to subar ¶ti ¶ r…ôgions, but tńß…ô gr…ôat…ôst ¶on ¶…ôntration of sp…ô ¶i…ôs di*…ôrsitj is in tropi ¶ar~…ĺ rainfor…ôsts. tńß…ôr…ô ar…ô appro*imat…ôr~…ĺj *,*** r…ô ¶ord…ôd sp…ô ¶i…ôs, a ¶ ¶ountiŇč for o*…ôr **% of …ô*tant ampńßibian sp…ô ¶i…ôs. tńß…ô ar…ô ar~…ĺso on…ô of tńß…ô fi*…ô most di*…ôrs…ô *…ôrt…ôbrat…ô ord…ôrs.
